Title: Molecular mechanisms driving the unusual pigmentation shift during eggplant fruit development

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Eggplant/Arabidopsis precursor used in some OE constructs
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of anthocyanin accumulation and positive indirect promoter of earlier naringenin chalcone/flavonol accumulation during fruit development.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. miRNA157 abundance increases during fruit development. 2. It cleaves SmSPL6a, SmSPL10, and SmSPL15 transcripts. 3. Overexpression in eggplant reduces early anthocyanins, causes whiter fruit, and shifts accumulation earlier toward naringenin chalcone/flavonols. 4. Its promoter is suppressed by SPL6a/10 and mildly activated by MYB12, indicating feedback regulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Small RNA-seq; qPCR; dual-luciferase cleavage assay; stable overexpression in eggplant; RNA-seq; metabolite profiling; promoter trans-activation assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low early (3 WPA), gradually increases and peaks late (11 WPA)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing miRNA157 activity may help prolong dark-purple fruit coloration, an agronomic and post-harvest quality trait.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression caused bushy architecture, delayed flowering, and altered reproductive development.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper emphasizes miRNA157 rather than miRNA156 as the functionally characterized principal miRNA in eggplant fruit pigmentation shift.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S in stable OE; endogenous promoter in trans-activation assay

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative post-transcriptional regulator of SmMYB12, thereby attenuating flavonol/naringenin chalcone-associated metabolism.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. miRNA858 shows negative correlation with SmMYB12 during fruit maturation. 2. It cleaves SmMYB12 transcripts in dual-luciferase assays. 3. Co-expression with SmMYB12 suppresses MYB12-induced flavonoid gene expression and metabolite accumulation in N. benthamiana. 4. Its promoter is activated by SPL6a/10 and mildly by MYB12, indicating feedback regulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Small RNA-seq; qPCR; dual-luciferase cleavage assay; transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; metabolite profiling; promoter trans-activation assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High earlier and declines during fruit development, opposite to MYB12
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suppressing miRNA858 may enhance flavonol/yellow pigment accumulation via MYB12 derepression.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May cause broader phenylpropanoid reprogramming due to MYB12 release.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of a feedback loop where MYB12 induces miRNA858 transcription while miRNA858 represses MYB12.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Tomato Ubq10 in transient OE assays; endogenous promoter in trans-activation assay

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a key branch-point enzyme promoting flavonol/naringenin chalcone-associated flux during late fruit development; activated by SmMYB12 and associated with increased yellow pigmentation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts dihydroflavonols into flavonols, directing flavonoid flux away from anthocyanin synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmFLS promoter was activated by SmMYB12 in transient trans-activation assays. 2. SmMYB12 was the only tested MYB that activated uprSmFLS. 3. The study identifies SmFLS as a crucial branch point underlying the pigmentation shift toward yellow flavonoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter trans-activation assay; transcript profiling; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Early biosynthetic genes including SmFLS were induced during fruit development and ripening, especially later stages.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering flavonol/yellow pigment enrichment in fruit skin, especially in combination with MYB12 modulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the paper explicitly highlights it as the crucial branch point for flavonol metabolism regulated by MYB12.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shifting flux toward flavonols may reduce anthocyanin accumulation and dark-purple coloration.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of anthocyanin biosynthesis in early fruit skin; its decline during development contributes to reduced purple pigmentation and the shift toward yellow flavonoids.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SBP/SPL) transcription factor
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; transcriptional repression within feedback loop; post-transcriptional repression by miRNA157
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis and pigmentation phase transition
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SmMYB1; SmMYB2; SmDFR; SmANS; miRNA157; miRNA858
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SmMYB1 and SmMYB2 promoters; activates SmDFR and SmANS promoters; suppresses miRNA157 promoter; activates miRNA858 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller and positive regulator required for early anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq/qPCR; dual-luciferase miRNA cleavage assay; promoter trans-activation assay; transient overexpression/metabolite profiling; miRNA157 overexpression in eggplant
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmSPL6a is targeted post-transcriptionally by miRNA157. 2. Its expression is high early and declines during fruit development. 3. It activates SmMYB1/2 and anthocyanin-pathway promoters, thereby promoting anthocyanin accumulation. 4. It suppresses miRNA157 and activates miRNA858, forming feedback regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback with miRNA157; contributes to feedforward activation of anthocyanin biosynthesis via MYB1/2; activates miRNA858
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in early fruit development, reduced during ripening; downregulated in miRNA157-OE lines especially at 3 WPA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for prolonging dark-purple fruit coloration and manipulating post-harvest quality by reducing miRNA157 activity or maintaining SPL activity.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation of miR156/157-SPL module alters vegetative/reproductive development, including bushy architecture and delayed flowering.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: No positive interaction detected with SmMYB1; SmMYB2; SmMYB12
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No evidence for SPL-MYB protein complex in eggplant from Y2H and LCI assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contrary to Arabidopsis/blueberry models, SmSPL6a positively rather than negatively regulates anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S in miRNA-OE studies; endogenous promoters in trans-activation assays

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of anthocyanin biosynthesis in early fruit skin; its reduction during development contributes to pigment shift away from anthocyanins.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SBP/SPL) transcription factor
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; transcriptional repression within feedback loop; post-transcriptional repression by miRNA157
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis and pigmentation phase transition
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SmMYB1 and SmMYB2 promoters; activates SmDFR promoter; suppresses miRNA157 promoter; activates miRNA858 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive upstream controller of early anthocyanin production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq/qPCR; dual-luciferase miRNA cleavage assay; promoter trans-activation assay; transient co-expression/metabolite profiling; miRNA157 overexpression in eggplant
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmSPL10 is a miRNA157 target. 2. It promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis by activating SmMYB1/2 and SmDFR. 3. It participates in feedback regulation by repressing miRNA157 and activating miRNA858. 4. In co-expression assays it enhanced MYB-mediated activation of anthocyanin output.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback with miRNA157; activates miRNA858; feedforward activation of anthocyanin pathway via MYB1/2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High at early developmental stage and declines later; reduced in miRNA157-OE fruit skin
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for maintaining purple peel color in breeding or engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation through miR156/157 pathway may alter architecture and flowering.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: No positive interaction detected with SmMYB1; SmMYB2; SmMYB12
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike SPLs in several model species, SmSPL10 is not an anthocyanin repressor.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of early anthocyanin accumulation and contributor to developmental pigmentation shift.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SBP/SPL) transcription factor
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; post-transcriptional repression by miRNA157
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis and developmental pigment switching
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SmMYB1 and SmMYB2 promoters; weakly activates SmMYB12 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of anthocyanin-promoting MYBs during early fruit development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq/qPCR; dual-luciferase miRNA cleavage assay; promoter trans-activation assay; transient co-expression/metabolite profiling; miRNA157 overexpression in eggplant
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmSPL15 is targeted by miRNA157. 2. It activates SmMYB1 and SmMYB2 promoters and weakly activates SmMYB12. 3. Co-expression with MYBs can maintain or enhance anthocyanin production. 4. Its decline during ripening coincides with anthocyanin loss.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of miRNA157-SPL feedback module; activates miRNA858 less clearly than SPL6a/10 and does not significantly suppress miRNA157 promoter in assay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High early and reduced later; decreased in miRNA157-OE lines at early stage
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for prolonging dark fruit skin coloration, though pathway manipulation may affect development.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Developmental pleiotropy likely through miR156/157-SPL network.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: No positive interaction detected with SmMYB1; SmMYB2; SmMYB12
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No evidence for direct SPL-MYB complex
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SmSPL15 also suppressed kaempferol metabolism in transient N. benthamiana assays.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of anthocyanin biosynthesis; promotes early purple pigmentation in fruit skin.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SmF3H; SmF35H; SmDFR; SmANS; Sm3GT1; SmCHI; SmC3H
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates promoters of SmF3H, SmF35H, SmDFR, SmANS, Sm3GT1, and SmCHI; represses SmC3H promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct activator of late biosynthetic genes driving anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in N. benthamiana; stable overexpression in S. nigrum; promoter trans-activation assays; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmMYB1 expression declines during fruit development in parallel with anthocyanin loss. 2. It induces anthocyanin accumulation in N. benthamiana and S. nigrum. 3. It activates multiple anthocyanin structural gene promoters. 4. It is transcriptionally activated by SmSPL6a/10/15.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: SmSPL6a; SmSPL10; SmSPL15; fruit developmental stage
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedforward component downstream of SPLs activating anthocyanin structural genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High early in fruit skin and reduced during ripening
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for enhancing purple anthocyanin pigmentation in eggplant or related Solanum fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May reduce flavonol flux and yellow pigmentation due to stronger pull toward anthocyanin branch.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: No positive interaction detected with SmSPL6a; SmSPL10; SmSPL15
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No direct interaction with SPLs detected in this study
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study treated SmMYB1 as a core anthocyanin activator in the developmental switch.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of anthocyanin biosynthesis in fruit and flowers; overexpression deepens purple color, while silencing causes pale/white tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Most tested flavonoid pathway gene promoters except SmC3H; Sm4CL2; SmFLS; also SmANS in co-activation assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances expression of most anthocyanin/flavonoid biosynthetic genes; activates multiple pathway promoters
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong positive regulator of anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in N. benthamiana; stable overexpression in S. nigrum; stable OE and RNAi in eggplant; transcript analysis; metabolite profiling; promoter trans-activation assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmMYB2 expression decreases during ripening similarly to SmMYB1. 2. Overexpression induces anthocyanins in N. benthamiana and earlier anthocyanin accumulation in S. nigrum fruit. 3. Eggplant SmMYB2-OE plants show deep purple flowers and fruit, whereas RNAi lines are pale/white. 4. It activates many flavonoid structural genes and functions downstream of SPLs.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: SmSPL6a; SmSPL10; SmSPL15; fruit developmental stage
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedforward activation of anthocyanin pathway downstream of SPLs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher early in fruit development, lower later
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; overexpression; RNAi silencing
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to intensify purple pigmentation in eggplant fruit and flowers.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May suppress yellow/flavonol branch and alter tissue color broadly.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: No positive interaction detected with SmSPL6a; SmSPL10; SmSPL15
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SmMYB2 was newly identified here as an anthocyanin activator complementing SmMYB1.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Tomato Ubq10 for OE constructs; RNAi vector in stable eggplant

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of naringenin chalcone/flavonol accumulation during later fruit development; promotes yellow pigmentation module.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; post-transcriptional repression by miRNA858
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Early flavonoid biosynthesis and flavonol branch activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates promoters of Sm4CL2, SmCHI, and SmFLS; mildly activates miRNA858 and miRNA157 promoters
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key inducer of the late developmental yellow-pigment module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in N. benthamiana; co-expression with miRNA858; metabolite profiling; qPCR; promoter trans-activation assays; dual-luciferase miRNA cleavage assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmMYB12 is post-transcriptionally targeted by miRNA858. 2. Overexpression induces naringenin, kaempferol, and quercetin accumulation and upregulates endogenous flavonoid genes in N. benthamiana. 3. It activates Sm4CL2, SmCHI, and uniquely SmFLS promoters. 4. It participates in feedback by activating miRNA858 and miRNA157 promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: miRNA858; SmSPL6a; SmSPL15 (weak promoter activation); fruit developmental stage
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback with miRNA858 and possible broader autoregulatory loop via MYB12-induced miRNA858; also activates miRNA157
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Increases later in fruit development, opposite to miRNA858
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for boosting flavonol/yellow pigmentation and nutritionally relevant flavonoids in fruit skin.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced MYB12 activity may reduce anthocyanin-associated dark color by shifting flux toward EBG/flavonol branch.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: No positive interaction detected with SmSPL6a; SmSPL10; SmSPL15
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central regulator of the yellow pigmentation program associated with ripe fruit skin.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Tomato Ubq10 in transient OE assays
